title: Health security : the President's report to the American people
content:
# Health security : the President's report to the American people

Creator: Domestic Policy Council (U.S.)
Date: 1993-01-01T00:00:00Z
Downloads: 26
Subjects: Health care reform -- United States, Health care reform, Health Care Reform -- United States, National Health Programs -- United States, United States

136 pages : 22 cm The official report by the President of the U.S. Lays out the dimensions of the health care crisis that confronts the U.S., explains its elements and complexities, and states the case for comprehensive reform as proposed in the Health Security Act. Six basic principles of reform are proposed: security, simplicity, savings, quality, choice, and responsibility. Includes "scenarios under reform". Charts and tables "The official text"--Cover "A Touchstone book." Letter from President William Jefferson Clinton -- Foreword by First Lady Hillary Rodham Clinton -- Why Change -- Rising Insecurity -- Growing Complexity -- Rising Costs -- Decreasing Quality -- Declining Choices -- Growing Irresponsibility -- An American Challenge -- Principles of Reform -- Security -- Simplicity -- Savings -- Quality -- Choice -- Responsibility -- How the New System Works -- How Reform Will Affect You -- Small Business in the New System -- Discounts for the Smallest Companies -- An Overview of the New System -- Flexibility -- Security -- Comprehensive Benefits -- Insurance Reform -- Limits on What Consumers and Businesses Pay -- Protecting Older Americans -- Access to Care in Rural and Urban Areas -- Simplicity -- Reducing Paperwork -- Cutting Red Tape -- Savings -- Increasing Competition -- Strengthening Buying Clout -- Lowering Administrative Costs -- Limiting Premium Increases -- Reducing Health Care Fraud -- Quality -- Better Information for Judging Quality -- Investing in Research -- Emphasizing Preventive Care -- Choice -- Choosing a Doctor -- Choosing a Health Plan -- Increasing Options for Long-Term Care -- Responsibility -- Medical Malpractice -- Paying for Health Security -- Conclusion -- Speech to Joint Session of Congress -- President William Jefferson Clinton -- September 22, 1993
